meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for AI Builders: Move Beyond Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building in the dark. Learn to implement professional analytics to track user behavior and graduate from basic apps to production-ready AI software.
content.answer_block: To break through the intermediate plateau, integrate event-based analytics. By tracking user friction points and LLM latency, you transform your project from a static demo into a data-driven product. Use Antigravity to instrument your codebase and Claude Code to automate the integration, turning raw usage into actionable growth insights.
content.intro:
You’ve built the MVP. It works on your machine, the UI is clean, and the AI responses are clever. Yet, you feel stuck in the 'tutorial loop,' unable to see how real users actually interact with your creation. Without data, you are flying blind, guessing which features matter and which are just technical debt in disguise.

True engineering isn't just about writing code; it’s about observing how that code performs in the wild. This guide bridges the gap between a student project and a professional SaaS by teaching you how to instrument your application. Let’s move past the basics and start building with the precision of a lead developer.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the specific user actions that indicate value, such as 'prompt_submitted' or 'feature_toggled'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 5 high-impact user events to track for an AI SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify key user interaction points in main.py and layout.tsx'

steps.title: Instrument the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Initialize your analytics SDK to capture events asynchronously without blocking the main execution thread.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, singleton-pattern analytics utility module using PostHog or Segment, optimized for Next.js.

steps.title: Capture LLM Performance Latency
steps.description: Track the time-to-first-token and total generation duration to detect bottlenecks in your AI pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a wrapper function for my OpenAI API calls that automatically logs latency and token usage to the analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'wrap LLM calls in analytics-logger.ts'

steps.title: Implement User Session Tracking
steps.description: Correlate individual events into a session timeline to visualize the user journey from landing page to conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a session tracking middleware that captures user unique IDs and device properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'add session context to all event payloads'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Sync your tracking events with a dashboard to visualize user flow and drop-off points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a query schema for visualizing the funnel between 'initial-prompt' and 'final-output' in the analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify event schema matches dashboard requirements'

pro_tips: Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your events; always hash user IDs before sending.
Track 'failed' attempts explicitly; knowing where your AI fails is more valuable than knowing where it succeeds.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to keep your analytics costs low during the early growth phase.
Tag events with the specific model version (e.g., gpt-4o vs gpt-4o-mini) to perform A/B testing on model quality.
Set up alerts for anomalous event volume, which is often the first sign of a breaking bug in production.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, spend 48 hours observing the data. Look for the 'aha!' moment where users get value, then optimize your UI to drive more users toward that specific action.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a product engineer. With these analytics in place, you can finally prove your app's value to users and employers alike. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
